meta_title: Impact Windows in Wyndham, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact windows in Wyndham, FL. Protect your home, lower insurance costs & meet Broward County codes. Free estimates!
h1: Impact Windows for Wyndham, FL Homes — Built to Withstand South Florida's Worst
intro:
Wyndham, Florida is one of Broward County's most desirable residential communities — and for good reason. Tucked between Coral Springs, Parkland, and Coconut Creek, this upper-middle-class enclave features well-maintained homes in neighborhoods like Wyndham Estates, where average property values hover around $480,000. Families here have invested significantly in their properties, their landscaping, and their quality of life. That investment deserves serious protection — and in a region where tropical systems form with little warning, impact windows are no longer a luxury. They're an essential layer of home defense.

Although Wyndham sits approximately 12 miles from the Atlantic coastline, don't let that inland distance lull you into a false sense of security. Broward County's wind zone designation for this area calls for construction standards capable of withstanding sustained winds of 140 mph. When a major hurricane tracks across or near South Florida, it doesn't discriminate between beachfront properties and inland subdivisions. Storm-force winds, wind-driven rain, and dangerous airborne debris can devastate neighborhoods across the entire county — as residents of this region learned firsthand during Hurricanes Andrew, Wilma, and Irma. Impact windows represent the single most effective upgrade you can make to defend against all three of those threats simultaneously.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we understand what Wyndham homeowners value: craftsmanship, reliability, and a team that respects your home as much as you do. Our crews have completed more than 500 homes across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, and we bring that same depth of experience to every project in Wyndham Estates and the surrounding areas. Whether your home was built before 1994 — when Florida's building codes were dramatically overhauled in the aftermath of Hurricane Andrew — or it's a newer construction that simply deserves upgraded glazing, we have the products, the expertise, and the service-first mindset to deliver results you'll rely on for decades.

section_why.h2: Why Wyndham Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
section_why.body:
Choosing an impact window contractor isn't just about finding a competitive price — it's about finding a team you can trust to do the job correctly the first time, using products that will truly perform when a storm arrives. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has built its reputation across South Florida as 'The Kings of Service,' and that title isn't self-appointed. It's the result of 60+ years of combined installation experience, an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau, and a company culture that puts quality craftsmanship above all else.

One of the key differentiators that sets Premier Impact Windows & Roofing apart from the competition is our 100% in-house installation model. We never subcontract your project to third-party crews whose training, experience, and accountability we cannot fully control. Every technician who shows up at your Wyndham home is a trained, background-checked member of our own team — someone who understands our installation standards, the specific requirements of Broward County's permitting process, and the nuances of working in established neighborhoods like Wyndham Estates where HOA guidelines and architectural consistency matter. When you hire Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you get Premier Impact Windows & Roofing from start to finish.

For Wyndham homeowners, local knowledge is everything. We're familiar with the construction styles common to this ZIP code (33076), the permit expectations of the Broward County Building Division, and the specific wind load requirements that apply to this area. Our project managers handle all permitting and inspection coordination on your behalf, so you're never left navigating bureaucratic red tape alone. From your initial free consultation through final inspection sign-off, our team manages the entire process with transparency and professionalism.

With over 500 completed homes across the tri-county area and financing options starting as low as $87 per month with $0 down, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes it accessible for virtually any Wyndham homeowner to upgrade to certified impact windows — without compromising on quality, brand, or long-term protection. We're not a high-pressure sales organization. We're neighbors who take the safety of South Florida families personally.

section_history.h2: Storm History That Proves Wyndham Needs More Than Standard Windows
section_history.body:
Wyndham and the broader Broward County region have been tested by major storms at least 8 times since 1990, and three of those events left lasting marks on how South Florida approaches home construction and storm preparedness. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 was the event that changed everything. Though its landfall was in Miami-Dade County, Andrew's catastrophic destruction — which left more than 175,000 people homeless — exposed shocking deficiencies in Florida's construction standards. Homes with inadequate windows and doors failed spectacularly, allowing wind to pressurize interiors and blow off roofs entirely. Approximately 25% of Wyndham-area homes were built before 1994, meaning they were constructed under the pre-Andrew building code era. Many of those properties still have their original windows, making them particularly vulnerable.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 brought a different kind of threat to Broward County — a fast-moving, powerful storm that generated significant wind damage across the entire county, including inland communities well away from the coast. Wyndham-area residents experienced power outages lasting weeks, structural damage to roofing and siding, and the kind of windborne debris impacts that shatter standard plate glass windows and allow water intrusion to destroy interiors. More recently, Hurricane Irma in 2017 served as a grim reminder that even a storm that weakens before reaching Broward can still produce the sustained winds and gusts capable of compromising homes that lack proper hurricane protection.

The pattern is unmistakable: South Florida storms don't wait for perfect conditions, and they don't spare inland neighborhoods. For homeowners in Wyndham Estates and throughout the 33076 ZIP code, impact-rated windows are the most direct response to a documented and recurring threat. Unlike storm shutters that must be installed before each storm and removed afterward, impact windows provide 365-days-a-year protection without any action required on your part when a storm watch is issued.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Windows in Broward County
section_code.body:
Wyndham falls within Broward County's jurisdiction, and all window replacement projects in this area are subject to the Florida Building Code (FBC) — one of the most rigorous residential construction standards in the nation. While Wyndham is not located within the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), which applies to Miami-Dade and Broward coastal communities, it is still required to meet strict hurricane-rated product standards. Any window installed in a Broward County home must be tested and certified to withstand the wind pressures associated with a 140 mph design wind speed — the minimum threshold for this wind zone designation.

This means that not just any 'impact window' will satisfy code. Products must carry documented testing certifications from accredited laboratories, including compliance with ASTM E1886 and ASTM E1996 impact resistance standards, as well as applicable wind load pressure ratings for your specific home's geometry and location. The Broward County Building Division reviews and approves all permit applications for window replacements, and inspectors will verify that installed products match the specifications on the approved permit drawings. Shortcuts and non-compliant installations are caught during inspection — and the cost of removal and reinstallation falls on the homeowner if work doesn't pass.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permitting process with the Broward County Building Division on your behalf. Our team prepares the required documentation, submits permit applications, coordinates inspection scheduling, and ensures every installation meets or exceeds the applicable FBC requirements. For homes built before 1994 — a significant portion of the Wyndham housing stock — we also perform a thorough assessment to ensure that the existing rough openings and structural framing can support the new certified units. Our goal is not just to pass inspection, but to ensure your windows will truly perform as rated when the next major storm makes landfall.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Windows: The Products We Trust for Wyndham Homes
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two industry-leading product lines that are ideally suited to the wind zone requirements and aesthetic expectations of Wyndham homeowners: Eastern Storm Windows (ESW) and CWI. Both brands are built specifically for South Florida's demanding climate conditions and carry the certifications required for permitted installation under Florida Building Code in Broward County.

ESW's Eastern Storm Windows line is engineered with multi-chamber frame profiles that maximize thermal and structural performance simultaneously. The laminated glass units feature a durable PVB (polyvinyl butyral) interlayer that holds the glass together even when struck by high-velocity debris — a critical feature in a region where projectiles ranging from tree branches to roofing tiles become airborne in major storms. Low-E (low-emissivity) glass coatings are standard in ESW products, reducing solar heat gain and UV transmission, which translates directly into lower air conditioning costs during Wyndham's long, sun-intense summers. For a community where homes average $480,000 in value, the sleek, modern profiles of ESW windows also provide a genuine aesthetic upgrade that complements architectural styles from Mediterranean revival to contemporary.

CWI products offer Wyndham homeowners an equally compelling option, with a full range of single-hung, double-hung, casement, and picture window configurations to accommodate virtually any residential design. CWI's frames are engineered for minimal visible sightlines, maximizing natural light while maintaining the structural integrity required for 140 mph wind zone performance. Both ESW and CWI products are tested to ASTM impact standards and carry the Florida Product Approvals (FPAs) necessary for Broward County permitted installations. Our installation teams are factory-trained on both product lines, ensuring that every unit is set, sealed, and secured precisely according to manufacturer specifications — which is essential not only for code compliance, but for warranty validity.

section_cost.h2: What Do Impact Windows Cost for a Wyndham Home — And How Do You Finance Them?
section_cost.body:
For homeowners in Wyndham Estates and the broader 33076 ZIP code, the investment in impact windows varies based on several factors: the number and size of openings, the specific product configuration selected, and the complexity of the installation (including any structural modifications needed for older pre-1994 homes). As a general benchmark, Wyndham homeowners in the $480,000 average value range are typically looking at a whole-home impact window project between $15,000 and $40,000, depending on home size and product selection. That figure represents a meaningful but well-justified investment when you account for the multi-dimensional return it delivers.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes that investment accessible through flexible financing options, including plans starting at just $87 per month with $0 down for qualified applicants. Our financing partners offer terms that allow Wyndham homeowners to begin protecting their families and properties immediately — often before the next hurricane season — without depleting savings or delaying other important projects. Given that impact windows add measurable resale value to homes in a market as competitive as Broward County, many homeowners find that the upgrade effectively pays for itself in home equity appreciation alone, without even accounting for energy savings or insurance reductions.

The return on investment calculation for impact windows in Wyndham is compelling. Energy savings from Low-E glass and improved insulation can reduce annual HVAC costs by 15–25% in South Florida's climate. Insurance premium reductions (discussed in detail in the next section) can generate savings of hundreds to over a thousand dollars per year. And the elimination of storm shutter installation labor before each storm event — which can cost $500–$1,500 annually if you hire professionals — adds yet another line item to the ROI equation. Over a 10-year horizon, the financial case for impact windows in Wyndham is exceptionally strong.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings for Wyndham Homeowners After Impact Window Installation
section_insurance.body:
One of the most tangible and immediate financial benefits of installing impact windows in Wyndham is the reduction in homeowners insurance premiums. Florida's insurance market is notoriously challenging — carriers have exited the state in large numbers, and those that remain have increased premiums significantly across Broward County over the past several years. Impact windows are one of the few home improvements that directly, measurably reduce your insurance carrier's risk exposure — and most carriers reward that reduction with significant premium credits.

After impact window installation, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing recommends that Wyndham homeowners schedule a wind mitigation inspection with a licensed Florida inspector. This inspection results in a standardized wind mitigation report (OIR-B1-1802 form) that documents the opening protection features of your home. When submitted to your insurance carrier, this report can generate credits that reduce the wind portion of your homeowners premium — which in Broward County typically represents 40–70% of your total policy cost. Depending on your carrier and coverage level, annual savings of $800 to $2,500 are realistic for Wyndham homes, with savings being most dramatic for homes that previously had no opening protection at all.

Major carriers actively writing policies in the Broward County market — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and Heritage Insurance — all recognize wind mitigation credits derived from impact window installation. For Wyndham homeowners on the Citizens policy, in particular, the premium reduction from a fully protected home (all openings with impact-rated glazing) can be substantial. We strongly encourage every Premier Impact Windows & Roofing customer to contact their insurance agent immediately upon installation completion and to share their wind mitigation report proactively. Many homeowners discover that the annual insurance savings alone bring the effective payback period for their impact windows down to seven years or fewer.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact windows in Wyndham if I'm 12 miles from the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. While Wyndham's inland location may reduce some storm surge risk, it offers no meaningful protection against hurricane-force winds and wind-driven debris. Broward County designates this area within a 140 mph wind zone, and storms like Wilma (2005) and Irma (2017) produced damaging winds well inland throughout the county. Impact windows protect against all forms of storm damage — wind, debris, and rain intrusion — regardless of your distance from the shoreline.

faqs.q: How much do impact windows cost for an average Wyndham home, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: For a typical Wyndham home in the $480,000 average value range, a whole-home impact window project generally runs between $15,000 and $40,000 depending on home size, window count, and product selection. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down for qualified applicants. We'll provide a detailed, no-obligation quote during your free consultation so you can make an informed decision without any pressure.

faqs.q: What permits are required for impact window installation in Broward County, and who handles them?
faqs.a: All impact window replacements in Wyndham require a permit from the Broward County Building Division. Permit applications must include product approval documentation, site-specific engineering if required, and installation drawings. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on your behalf — from application submission through final inspection scheduling. Our team is well-versed in Broward County's requirements and ensures every installation is fully permitted and code-compliant from day one.

faqs.q: How long does it take to install impact windows throughout a Wyndham home?
faqs.a: For most Wyndham homes, the actual window installation takes one to three days depending on the number of openings and complexity of the project. However, the full timeline from initial consultation to final inspection typically spans four to eight weeks, with permit processing being the primary variable. Our project managers keep you informed at every stage and work to move your project through the approval process as efficiently as possible so your home is protected well ahead of hurricane season.

faqs.q: Will impact windows really lower my homeowners insurance premium in Wyndham?
faqs.a: Yes — in most cases, significantly. After installation, a licensed wind mitigation inspector documents your new opening protection and produces a report submitted to your insurance carrier. For Wyndham homes in Broward County, this wind mitigation credit can reduce the wind portion of your premium by 20–45% or more, depending on your carrier and prior protection level. Annual savings of $800–$2,500 are common, which meaningfully shortens the payback period on your impact window investment.
